blocks.text: SRS Airbag System Operational Precautions

Before carrying out any inspection or maintenance work on the SRS airbag system (driver/front passenger airbag, seat belt pretensioner, side airbags and side curtain airbags), be sure to read the following information carefully and proceed according to this manual.
This vehicle uses a DENSO or TRW SRS unit depending on the specifications; when performing work related to the SRS unit, use HDS to confirm the ECU manufacturer displayed in the data list.

items: Failure to follow the correct procedures and methods may result in malfunctions that may cause injury or prevent the system from functioning properly.

items.text: The SRS airbag system inspection and removal work shall be performed after the ignition is turned to the OFF (LOCK) mode, the cables are disconnected from the minus (-) terminal of the 12V battery, and at least 3 minutes have elapsed. Also, do not connect the cable to the minus (-) terminal of the 12V battery until the work is completed. (except for special instructions in the failure diagnosis)

Within 3 minutes after the ignition is turned to the OFF (LOCK) mode, the SRS unit, front impact sensors, and side impact sensors are still functional, and an impact may cause the airbags or other devices to deploy.

Within 3 minutes after the ignition is turned to the OFF (LOCK) mode, the SRS unit, front impact sensors, and side impact sensors are still functional, and an impact may cause the airbags or other devices to deploy.

The memory function of this system does not erase the memory contents of the SRS airbag system when the ignition is in the OFF (LOCK) mode or when the cable is disconnected from the negative (-) terminal of the 12V battery.
When removing parts of the SRS airbag system (including disconnecting couplers), the airbag insulator shall be disconnected first to prevent accidents due to malfunction.
When replacing parts of the SRS airbag system, replace them with new parts of the specified specifications.
Do not drop parts of the SRS airbag system. Inspect for dents, cracks, or deformation before installation, and replace if any abnormality is found.
SRS airbag systems should be inspected using a digital circuit tester that meets the standards.
Do not work with objects (testers, tools, etc.) on the passenger airbag.
Never disassemble it. Also, once activated (deployed), it cannot be reused.
Once activated (deployed), it must be replaced.

When storing the driver/front passenger airbag, side airbags and side curtain airbags separately, they must be stored with the padded side (deployed side) facing up (side airbags must be stored with the mounting side down). (Side airbags must be stored with the mounting side down.) Do not store them with the padded side down, even temporarily. Do not place any objects on the driver/front passenger airbag, side airbags, or side curtain airbags.
Do not allow oil, grease, washing oil, water, etc. to adhere to the product.
Store on a hard, flat surface away from light heat sources, avoiding high temperatures (above 93°C).
Never perform electrical inspections such as resistance measurements.
When removing, inspecting, or replacing airbags, do not do so in front of the airbags.

Disposal of driver/front-passenger airbags, seat belt pretensioners, side airbags and side curtain airbags must be done in accordance with the following guidelines Disposal of Airbag/Seat Belt Pretensioners/Side Airbags and Side Curtain Airbags. Dispose of the product in accordance with the following guidelines.

refs.label: Disposal of Airbag/Seat Belt Pretensioners/Side Airbags and Side Curtain Airbags.

items.text: Do not subject the SRS unit, front impact sensor and side impact sensor to any impact when the ignition is in the ON mode or within 3 minutes after the OFF (LOCK) mode.
Do not use an impact wrench when removing and installing the product. Also, do not give any strong impact to the vicinity.
Never disassemble.

The SRS unit shall be detached and the coupler connected to the SRS unit shall be disconnected after the ignition is turned to the OFF (LOCK) mode, the cable is disconnected from the minus (-) terminal of the 12V battery, and at least 3 minutes have elapsed.
Installation shall be tightened to the specified torque.
Store the product away from oil, water, and dust.
Store in a non-condensing location with a temperature of 40°C or less and a humidity of 80% or less.
Yellow couplers and insulators are used in the SRS airbag system and are identified with wire harnesses/couplers and insulators in other systems.
In the event of damage or cut, be sure to replace the product with a new one without soldering or other repairs.

If the side sill (outer/inner side) to which the side impact sensor is attached is deformed during a side impact to the side of the vehicle, or if the seat is found to be deformed, the associated wire harness should be replaced with a new one.
Install the product in such a way that it does not bite into or interfere with other parts.

The grounding part must be securely attached to the body, making sure that there is no dirt or paint adhering to it. A poor grounding may cause the SRS airbag system to malfunction, so care must be taken.

Seats with side airbags are marked with "SIDE AIRBAG" (A) on the outer surface of the seatback. The specifications of the seat back trim cover and other components differ from those of vehicles without side airbags, so be careful not to install them incorrectly when replacing parts.
Do not wash the sheet by pouring water on it. Also, do not spray steam.
Seat back pad shall be replaced with a new one if cracked.
Seat frames shall be replaced if any deformation can be seen externally.
Be careful not to twist or damage the airbag because the airbag module consists of a flexible airbag connected to a heavy object such as an inflator or adapter pipe.
Holding bracket (A).
The cable reel shall be centered when performing the following tasks

refs.label: The cable reel shall be centered when performing the following tasks

items.text: When installing the steering wheel.
When installing the steering wheel.
When installing the cable reel.
When the rotational position relationship of the steering wheel is changed when the steering column or other steering system is detached or adjusted.

items: Failure to perform centering is very dangerous because the cable reel may break, causing the airbag system, horn, etc. to malfunction.

items.text: Do not disassemble or apply grease to the cable reel. If any abnormality is found (e.g., snagging), be sure to replace the reel with a new one.

blocks.text: How to disconnect
Hold the pin insulator (A) with one hand and the slider (B) with the other hand and pull in the direction of the arrow shown in the figure.
Do not attempt to disconnect the insulator with the socket insulator (C).
Connection method
Insert the socket insulator (B) into the pin insulator (A) and push the socket insulator until it clicks into place.
How to disconnect
Hold the pin insulator (A) with one hand and the cover insulator (B) with the other hand and pull in the direction of the arrow shown in the figure.

1. Insert the socket insulator (B) into the pin insulator (A) and press the cover insulator (D) into the tabs (C) of the pin insulator. Cover Do not hold the insulator. 2. When the socket insulator is pressed hard, the cover insulator slides over the tabs and is connected.
How to disconnect
Hold the slider (A) and pull it in the direction of the arrow shown in the figure.
Do not attempt to disconnect with the socket insulator (B).
Connection method
Insert the socket insulator (A) and push the socket insulator in until it clicks into place.
Do not try to connect the coupler with the slider (B).
SRS unit The following procedure shows how to disconnect and connect couplers A and B.
While pressing the lock tab (A), pull out the coupler to disconnect it.
When connecting, push the coupler all the way in until it clicks and locks securely.
SRS unit The following procedure shows how to disconnect and connect couplers A and B.
How to disconnect
Pull the lever (B) in the direction of the arrow while pressing the tab (A), and pull out the coupler (C) to disconnect it.
Connection method

1. Holding the cord side of the coupler (A), insert the terminal side of the coupler into the SRS unit (B). 2. When the coupler is pushed in, the sleeve on the SRS unit side pushes the lever (C) back in the direction of the arrow. At this time, push the coupler in without touching the lever. 3. Push the lever toward the SRS unit until it clicks and locks securely.
